paragraphs: DOC-26 | Version 1.0 | Meal Planning RE Technical Docs v1
Defines metrics, event taxonomy, dashboards, cohort-quality checks, onboarding funnel tracking, meal-class acceptance, add-on relevance, experimentation, and data-quality monitoring.

This document is part of the canonical 28-document technical system. It should be read in the order defined in DOC-00. It is written for an AI coding agent that must implement the agreed recommendation engine exactly, without inventing a competing architecture.
Always build a household profile before generating any plan.
Always map user-facing cohort answers into backend personas and overlays; do not expose all backend personas in onboarding.
Always generate a 7-day class-first plan before selecting dishes.
Always keep primary family meal classes separate from member-specific add-on components.
Never allow kid, elderly, pregnancy, postpartum, diabetic, or recovery add-on classes to replace the main family meal unless the household itself has only that member segment.
Always apply hard constraints before scoring: diet type, allergies, Jain/fasting rules, and Never list.
Always use home state and current city as separate signals; do not collapse them into one location field.
Always use class-to-dish mapping keyed by meal_class_code; never mix dishes from unrelated meal classes.
Use v3 workbook IDs as canonical seed IDs unless a later approved source-data version supersedes them.
Measure whether the system creates first-day WOW, improves with revealed behavior, avoids wrong constraints, and keeps data taxonomy healthy.
Read DOC-00 first to understand build sequence and document dependencies.
Use this document when implementing: BUILD-10.
Treat all IDs in source workbook v3 as canonical seed data identifiers.
When logic and data conflict, follow the rule hierarchy: hard constraints > add-on separation > weekly class matrix > state/city overlay > dish scoring > randomization.
Do not create new meal class codes, persona codes, or enum values in application code. Additions must go through DOC-27 Admin CMS and DOC-28 governance.
Write unit tests based on the acceptance criteria in this document and DOC-25.
Experiments do not violate hard constraints.
tables: Defines metrics, event taxonomy, dashboards, cohort-quality checks, onboarding funnel tracking, meal-class acceptance, add-on relevance, experimentation, and data-quality monitoring.
Claude / AI coding agent, product owner, backend developer, data operations, QA
Generate meal classes first; expand to dishes second; attach member add-ons separately.
Track funnel, plan quality, acceptance, add-on relevance, class accuracy, taxonomy changes, and A/B tests.
Percent of first suggested plan/cards kept or locked.
Add-on accepted/used by target member segment.
Initial canonical technical document generated from agreed v3 workbook and documentation architecture.
